documents.title: Demand Planning Launch Brief — Luminance Brightening Serum (Aura Collective)

sections.heading: Product & Project Summary
sections.content:
Product name: Luminance Brightening Serum
Client brand: Aura Collective
Category: Skincare — face serum
Project status: In formulation development — formula lock target week 8
Launch market: United States (primary); Canada +6 months

sections.heading: Launch Parameters
sections.content:
Target US launch date: Month 6 from project start
Distribution channels: DTC (Shopify-based, client-owned) — primary channel
Secondary channel: Potential boutique retail placement; no confirmed retail accounts yet
Packaging: 30mL glass dropper bottle, single SKU at launch

sections.heading: Volume Forecast
sections.content:
Initial stocking quantity: 5,000 units
Expected monthly run rate by month 6: 15,000–20,000 units
Year 1 volume estimate: ~120,000–150,000 units (assumes ramp from launch)
Forecast basis: Client sales projections based on existing DTC audience size and planned paid media spend
Note: This is a new brand launch — no historical sales data available. Forecast is marketing-model driven.

sections.heading: SKU Structure
sections.content:
SKUs at launch: 1 (30mL glass dropper)
Future variants: Client has mentioned potential 50mL size for retail — not confirmed, not in Year 1 plan
No secondary packaging variants planned at launch

sections.heading: Seasonality & Promotional Calendar
sections.content:
No strong seasonal pattern expected for a brightening serum
Known promotional events: Launch campaign planned (exact date TBD); Black Friday / Cyber Monday likely promotional push
Influencer seeding: Client plans pre-launch influencer program — may generate short-term demand spike 2–4 weeks post-launch

sections.heading: Supply Constraints
sections.content:
• Tranexamic acid: Specialty active — confirm supplier lead time with R&D; may require 4–6 week advance procurement
• Glass dropper bottle: Client-sourced primary packaging — confirm lead time with client; Planning to track independently
• Ascorbyl glucoside: Confirm availability and lead time with R&D during formulation
• MOQ: To be confirmed once formula is locked and BOM is finalized

sections.heading: Assumptions & Risks
sections.content:
Key assumptions:
• Client DTC audience conversion rate as projected — no validation data available
• Retail placement does not materialize in Year 1 (upside risk if it does)
• Formula lock achieved by week 8 — any delay directly compresses launch window

Top demand risks:
• New brand with no sales history — forecast confidence is low; plan for +/- 40% variance in months 1–3
• Influencer-driven spike may create short-term stockout risk if not buffered
• Retail pitch success (positive risk) could require rapid scale-up beyond current plan

sections.heading: Planning Actions Required
sections.content:
1. Confirm formula lock date from R&D (target week 8) to finalize production scheduling
2. Obtain BOM from R&D post-formula lock to assess raw material lead times
3. Coordinate with client on glass bottle packaging lead time and delivery schedule
4. Set initial safety stock policy — recommend 4-week cover given forecast uncertainty
5. Flag tranexamic acid as long-lead-time item to Procurement immediately
